ques: Determine the current top business story on BBC News and give a brief overview of its economic implications.
answer: The current top BBC business story is "Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels" — Brent crude dropped below ~$72/barrel as Strait of Hormuz shipping resumes after a US–Iran de-escalation deal. Economic implications: lower energy/input costs ease inflation, cheaper petrol/diesel (UK petrol set to fall below 150p/litre; US gasoline down to ~$3.93/gal) boost consumer spending power, and restored shipping flows reduce supply-chain risk premiums — but analysts warn the relief is fragile and prices could spike again if tensions reignite.
